articles.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
articles.summary: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his f

articles.title: North Korea says its latest weapons tests included missiles with cluster-bomb warheads
articles.summary: North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.

articles.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
articles.summary: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

articles.title: Teen birth rates hit another historical low in 2025, CDC says
articles.summary: The teen birth rate continues its decades-long downward trend. Researchers say many factors are at play, including less sexual activity and more access to contraception and abortion.

articles.title: Trump Finds Himself With Fragile Iran Cease-Fire After the Scramble to Make a Deal
articles.summary: After careening from one diplomatic extreme to another, President Trump finds himself with a fragile deal that is already showing signs of fraying.

articles.title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
articles.summary: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.

articles.title: Gilgo Beach Killer’s ‘Unusual’ Deal Could Help Solve Other Murders
articles.summary: Rex Heuermann agreed to talk to behavioral analysts at the F.B.I. when he pleaded guilty. The unit has interviewed Ted Bundy, John Wayne Gacy and Richard Speck.

articles.title: Federal Court Denies Anthropic’s Motion to Lift ‘Supply Chain Risk’ Label
articles.summary: The ruling was a setback for the artificial intelligence start-up in its battle with the Defense Department over the use of A.I. in warfare.

articles.title: Once seen as a war hero, Australian ex-soldier will stand trial for alleged war crimes
articles.summary: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.

articles.title: After Cease-Fire, Iranians Are Left to Pick Up the Pieces
articles.summary: Hours after a tenuous pause to a nearly six-week war, Iranians expressed relief and trepidation about the future. Some fear the government will crack down on its domestic critics.

articles.title: Bill Gates will testify in the Epstein probe; Pam Bondi testimony postponed
articles.summary: Bill Gates will appear before the House Oversight Committee in June. The Department of Justice said Wednesday that former Attorney General Pam Bondi will not testify for now.

articles.title: In 20 Minutes, Gilgo Beach Killer Admits to Murdering 8 Women
articles.summary: As he pleaded guilty on Wednesday, Rex Heuermann gave the same one-word answer each time he was asked how he killed one of his victims.

articles.title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
articles.summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

articles.title: What has the U.S. war with Iran accomplished?
articles.summary: Trump's war goals included putting an end to Iran's nuclear program, destroying its military capabilities and creating regime change. That hasn't happened.

articles.title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
articles.summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the po

articles.title: Why OpenAI bought 'SportsCenter for Silicon Valley'
articles.summary: OpenAI is seeking to shape the public narrative about AI with the purchase of a niche talk show popular with Silicon Valley insiders.

articles.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
articles.summary: Downtown Cairo, or Wust el-Balad as it's known, is a trove of hidden gems. Imprinted on every high-ceilinged building, arched balcony and iconic roundabout are relics that feel like love letters from

articles.title: Oil prices plunge and stocks soar after U.S. and Iran agree on a ceasefire
articles.summary: Investors around the world breathed a sigh of relief at the prospect of peace — and an easing of the global energy crisis.

articles.title: U.S., Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins House race
articles.summary: The U.S. reached a last-minute ceasefire with Iran just before Trump's deadline for the country to meet his demands. And, Trump-backed Clay Fuller wins the U.S. House race in Georgia.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
